rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical fluoromethylation (CF3/CF2H/CF2Cl) of N-allylbenzamides triggering a Truce-Smiles 1,4-aryl migration to give CF2X-containing β-arylethylamines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent) to CF3SO2 radical which loses SO2 to give CF3 radical; later a second anodic oxidation converts the carbamoyl radical D to acyl/carbamoyl cation E that is trapped by ROH

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical (from Langlois reagent after SO2 extrusion)
β-amino C-centered radical A (after CF3 addition to alkene)
spirocyclic 6-endo cyclohexadienyl radical C' (off-path dihydroisoquinolinone precursor)
ring-opened aryl-migrated carbamoyl/N-acyl radical C (after C-C cleavage, before decarbonylation)
carbamoyl radical D (after homolytic C-C cleavage)
carbamoyl cation E (after second anodic oxidation)
carbamate cation F / final carbamate product after ROH addition

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2Na to CF3SO2 radical which extrudes SO2 to give CF3 radical; CF3 adds regioselectively to the allyl alkene generating a β-amino radical that undergoes 5-exo ipso cyclization onto the benzamide arene, then C–C homolysis releases the migrated aryl and a carbamoyl radical, which is anodically oxidized to a carbamoyl cation trapped by ROH to yield the carbamate.
rate_determining_step: Ipso 5-exo cyclization of the β-amino radical onto the arene (TS_BC), competing with 6-endo cyclization; selectivity governed by N-protecting group (t-Bu favors 5-exo, ΔΔE‡ ≈ 3.1 kcal/mol vs 1.6 for Ts)
notes: DFT (B3LYP-D3/def2-SV(P) with DLPNO-CCSD(T)/def2-TZVPP single points) rationalizes why bulky t-Bu N-protection favors s-cis conformer of β-amino radical A enabling 5-exo ipso attack and 1,4-aryl migration, whereas Ts protection allows π-stacking that stabilizes s-trans and promotes competitive 6-endo cyclization to dihydroisoquinolinones. Alternative pathway: 6-endo cyclization gives dihydroisoquinolinones (observed as side products with Ts/OMe protecting groups). A putative carbocation oxidation side pathway from the β-amino radical is also drawn in Scheme 1c. No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT) or KIE explicitly reported in these pages.
